questionBank.question: Using definition, d/dx(x^2) equals:
questionBank.timeTip: For basics, recall power rule quickly.

questionBank.question: If f(1)=3 and f'(1)=5, equation of tangent line at x=1 is:
questionBank.explanation: Point-slope form with point (1,3) and slope 5.

questionBank.question: If f is differentiable at x=a, which is true?
d/dx[(x^2)(sin x)] =
questionBank.notesSnippet: Differentiate each factor once and add.
questionBank.timeTip: Keep terms in original order to avoid sign errors.

questionBank.question: d/dx[(x^2+1)/x] for x!=0 is:
questionBank.timeTip: Write u and v before differentiating.

parts.label: (b)
parts.question: Find tangent line to f at x=2.
parts.scoring: 1 pt point, 1 pt slope, 1 pt line.

2.1.title: 2-1 to 2-4: Derivative Foundations
2.1.content:
Derivative meaning

Derivative is instantaneous rate of change and slope of tangent line.

2.2.title: 2-5 to 2-7: Basic Rules
2.2.content:
Power + basic derivatives

Know derivatives of powers, trig basics, and exponentials.

2.3.title: 2-8 to 2-10: Product, Quotient, Trig
2.3.content:
Rule selection

Choose product/quotient rules when functions are multiplied/divided.
